mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic slowdown, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real sales of automobiles and light trucks alongside stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer spending power and accommodative financing, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, it signals entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 210.0 coincides with positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy when core inflation diverges from headline measures.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across labor and construction sectors, reinforcing economic resilience and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when HOUST declines despite PAYEMS growth, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate during a recessionary downturn signals immediate labor market distress, triggering defensive portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: UNRATE spikes fail to predict negative 5-day returns during recessionary periods, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when adjusted for market volatility regimes.
